task_completion.description: Access the SEB Server login page
task_completion.visual_evidence: A web browser is open to http://localhost:8080 showing the SEB Server login screen.
The SEB Server dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating successful login with the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the Client Configurations section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SEB Server interface shows the 'Client Configurations' or 'Clients' management page.
The client configuration named 'Loaner-Laptop-22' is visible in the list of clients.
Visual confirmation that 'Loaner-Laptop-22' is deactivated, such as a status toggle switched to off, an 'inactive' status badge, or a success message indicating the configuration was deactivated.

integrity.description: Agent used a web browser to interact with the SEB Server
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions are performed within a web browser interface, not via terminal commands like curl.

integrity.description: Agent did not use direct database manipulation to bypass the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal or database client (e.g., MySQL CLI) is used to run UPDATE queries on the seb_client_configuration table.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the genuine SEB Server application
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser displays the actual SEB Server web application at localhost:8080, not a local text file or mocked HTML page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target client configuration is named 'Loaner-Laptop-22'. The expected final status for this client is 'inactive'. The client is pre-injected into the system database, so the agent should find it already existing in the list and only needs to deactivate it.
